patch.value:
## Context
Every mutating action appends permanently to an item's hash-chained JSONL stream — by design, nothing is silently alterable afterwards. That invariant turns a pasted credential into a permanent artifact: the only remediation is the heavyweight recorded redaction workflow (history-redact), which rewrites and re-anchors the chain. The repo already has credential-shape rules (scripts/check-secrets.mjs, exported scanContent + RULES, hardened in pm-h4zb), but they only guard THIS repository's tracked files in CI. The product itself offers no protection: any agent on any pm workspace can pm create/update/append/notes/comments a live token and only discover the problem after it is chained.

## Proposal
Promote credential-shape detection to an SDK primitive and run it on the text-bearing mutation write path (create, update, append, close reasons, comments, notes, learnings):
- SDK: a pure scanContent(text) -> findings primitive with the existing rule set (GitHub token prefixes, private keys, AWS keys, generic high-entropy assignments) as the deterministic default, extension-overridable like other scorer/provider contracts.
- CLI/MCP: config-controlled mode secret_guard = advise (default; one-line stderr warning naming the rule + offset, mutation proceeds) | block (structured error, mutation refused, --allow-secret override for false positives) | off. Never-block principle: advise mode must add no perceptible latency and never fail the mutation on scanner errors.
- Error/advisory text points at the redaction workflow for content that already landed.

## Why now
The immutable-proof roadmap (portable attestation pm-3z0k, workspace stream pm-klo8, completeness gate pm-9yfo) keeps strengthening permanence guarantees; prevention at the write boundary is the missing complement. At thousands-of-agents scale a single leaked credential in a shared workspace is org-wide exposure.
Write-time secret detection advisory: mutation paths flag credential-shaped content before it enters the immutable history stream

SDK exports a documented secret-scan primitive with deterministic default rules and unit-tested detection for GitHub token prefixes, private keys, AWS keys, and high-entropy assignments; all text-bearing CLI+MCP mutation paths consult it honoring secret_guard advise/block/off config; advise emits a single stderr line and never alters exit code or output shape; block returns a structured error with an explicit override flag; scanner failure never blocks a mutation; docs cover configuration and the remediation path for already-recorded content

author: codex-root

patch.value: Add an SDK-first configurable pre-write secret scanner across text-bearing CLI and MCP mutations, with advisory, blocking, and disabled modes plus safe failure semantics.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Evidence 2026-07-24: shipped SDK-first redacted secret detection across CLI and MCP mutation preflight with off/advise/block modes and explicit force results. Clean packed-install acceptance discovered that generic CLI recovery initially echoed a credential-shaped argument; fixed it with compact redacted recovery metadata and added JSON+--explain regression coverage proving attempted_command and normalized_args never contain the value. Installed artifact blocks safely, SDK force override is explicit, linked tests passed, secret scan found no tracked credentials, and full 6208-test coverage is exact 100/100/100/100.

patch.value: Shared mutation secret guard now protects SDK, CLI, and MCP writes with redacted diagnostics and explicit policy/override semantics.
Credential-shaped content is detected before immutable history writes, configurable as advise/block/off, without leaking matched values or breaking safe mutations.
All configured modes and detectors ship through public SDK primitives; blocked CLI JSON/explain recovery is fully redacted and package-first acceptance passes.
Implemented and verified shared redacted mutation secret guard across SDK, CLI, and MCP including packed recovery-bundle leak regression.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Follow-up verification: credential-detector test fixtures now construct representative token/key shapes from fragments at runtime, so the tests still exercise exact detection semantics while the repository secret gate correctly rejects stored credential-shaped literals. Focused 51-test mutation/runtime set and tracked-file secret scan pass.
